acceptanceCriteria: TI-12 (mutation-score decline) moves from rejected to conditional shadow, restricted to repositories that already run a pinned mutation tool as a configured check with comparable scores over the same configuration and mutant set; no default mutation run is introduced; the falsifiable-tests row gains a must-fire/must-not-fire pair consistent with that scope; TI-12 is not described as implemented.

TI-13 and TI-15 move from advisory only to shadow candidate status that is explicitly PROPOSED and lower priority: the document distinguishes the implemented scanner set (TI-05..TI-09, collected by rigor test-integrity-scan per #22) from these proposed additions that the scanner does not yet evaluate, without renaming or weakening the implemented rung.

TI-13 and TI-15 confidence cells split into a deterministic configured-rule-match component (high) and an advisory weakening-interpretation component (low-medium); TI-11's confidence cell distinguishes measurement accuracy under identical tool, version, configuration, and scope from the advisory interpretation that a decline indicates weakening.

TI-01's false-positive-risk wording claims only detection of a configured-check definition change without inferring intent, and its must-not-fire case becomes an unrelated .rigor/policy.json edit that leaves the checks array untouched.

TI-08's must-not-fire case additionally covers a snapshot-only diff with no accompanying implementation changes, matching the implemented conjunctive detector in src/test-integrity.ts which returns null when either side of the conjunction is empty.
The catalog states explicitly that the implemented TI-02/TI-03/TI-04 gate constrains cross-attempt progress classification (reduced/incomparable), not verification, review, or merge outcomes.

All scenario tables, the enforcement ladder, the shadow-candidate/implemented-collection sections, and cross-references remain mutually consistent; every existing label (deterministic fact / tool-derived measurement / advisory interpretation) is unchanged; TI-16 remains rejected as an authoritative check and TI-10/TI-14 remain advisory only.

CHANGELOG.md documents the amendment under the existing Unreleased heading alongside the #15 entry, attributing the revisions to the recorded GH-21-R2 Codex consultation without describing Codex agreement as approval or verification.
npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict pass, and git diff --check is clean.
constraints: Documentation-only change: touch nothing outside docs/test-integrity.md and CHANGELOG.md (GH-21-R3 evidence under .rigor/evidence/ is written by Rigor commands, not the implementation agent).

No signal may be promoted to deterministic fact or given any enforcement effect; TI-12/TI-13/TI-15 must not be described as implemented or as collected by the current scanner; the implemented TI-05..TI-09 shadow collection and its record-only semantics must not be weakened or misdescribed.
Do not weaken or remove any existing scenario, falsifiable test, honesty disclaimer, the 'What must never be advertised' section, or any #45-introduced documentation of the implemented scanner.

Enforcement beyond shadow collection remains contingent on #22 evidence and #16 calibration followed by a separate human decision (#23); the amendment must not pre-commit any threshold or promotion rule.
